name: Pasta e Fagioli da Napoli!

ingredients.item: San Marzano tomatoes plus one can of water or beef broth
guanciale round cut into pieces or 4 Italian sausages
cans of cannelloni, kidney, or Italian bean mix
grams ditali pasta or you can use any small

method: In a stockpot on medium heat, add guanciale. You don't need to use any oil because the guanciale has so much fat, the excess fat a little like when you fry bacon. Fry until crispy, drain about half of the grease. (If using Italian sausage, add 1 T olive oil, (remove casings) and break up sausages into pieces with a wooden spoon until cooked through.) Remove a few pieces of guanciale from the frying pan to garnish later. In the same frying pan, add onion, celery, carrot, parsley, and crushed garlic (you can remove later), and saute for two minutes. Add 2 cans of beans, drained and rinsed well, can of tomatoes, water, and seasonings. Let simmer for 3 hours, as the water evaporates, add more water and stir every once in a while to prevent sticking. Near the end of your cooking time, prepare pasta, cook until al dente. Drain the pasta, and add to the beans and sauce and mix for a minute or two. Garnish with grated parmesan or pecorino and guanciale pieces. Serving suggestion: warm homemade bread and salad. Serves 2- 4. Olé
